So the right agent is in the gap, and it can move UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT within the gap. The purple bar is at cols 33-38, blocking movement to the right panel.
Let me think about what the goal could be:

2. Get both agents into the left c-frame? But the left c-frame only fits one agent.

The right agent is at rows 33-35, cols 21-23. The right c-frame is at rows 26-30, cols 44-48. The purple bar is at cols 33-38.

Wait, but the right agent was originally in the right panel (at cols 30-32 before I moved it LEFT). So the right agent can be in the right panel. The question is whether it can reach the right c-frame.
The right c-frame is at rows 26-30, cols 44-48. The right agent is currently at rows 33-35, cols 21-23. If the right agent could get back to the right panel, it could move UP to rows 27-29 and then RIGHT to cols 45-47.
But the purple bar blocks the path from the gap to the right panel. Unless... there's a way to bypass it.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. The right agent started in the right panel. I moved it LEFT through the gap. The purple bar is between the gap and the right panel. But the right agent was able to move LEFT from the right panel into the gap, so the purple bar doesn't block LEFT movement. Does it block RIGHT movement?
